claimText: The Climate Risk Index (CRI) is published by Germanwatch.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the publishing agency of the index.

claimText: The Climate Risk Index (CRI) ranks countries based on the human and economic toll of extreme weather events.
evidenceExcerpt: Ranks countries based on human and economic toll of extreme weather events.
examUse: Statement-based question on the methodology and parameters of the index.
distractorHooks: Ranks based on greenhouse gas emissions
Ranks based on renewable energy transition
Ranks based on climate policy implementation

claimText: The Climate Risk Index (CRI) was introduced in 2006 as an annual global climate impact index.
evidenceExcerpt: Introduced in 2006 as an annual global climate impact index.
examUse: Statement-based question on the history and frequency of the index.
distractorHooks: Introduced after the Paris Agreement in 2015

claimText: India ranked 15th in the Climate Risk Index (CRI) 2024 and 9th for the period 1995-2024.
evidenceExcerpt: India ranked 15th in CRI 2024 and 9th in CRI for 1995-2024.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's performance in global climate indices.
distractorHooks: India is not included in the index
